name: Pink Fairy Armadillo
primary_sources: IUCN Red List of Threatened Species

visual_attributes.morphology: small, elongated body with a flexible dorsal shell
visual_attributes.texture: silky fur with a leathery shell
visual_attributes.structural_arrangement: shell attached by thin dorsal membranes, 24 bands on shell

domain_specific_visual.dorsal_shell: flexible and soft, attached by a thin membrane

domain_specific_functional.burrowing: uses specialized claws to dig quickly
domain_specific_functional.thermoregulation: fur and shell help conserve heat

relationships.description: Both belong to the subfamily Chlamyphorinae and share morphological similarities.
The species is found exclusively in the deserts and scrublands of central Argentina.

contextual_attributes.geographic_range: Deserts and scrublands of central Argentina
contextual_attributes.cultural_significance: Represents unique evolutionary adaptations to desert environments
primary_sources: IUCN Red List of Threatened Species
